title: Temporal Coverage
description: Temporal coverage of an entity, expressible in CE years, AH (Hijri) years, and / or ISO 8601 dates. Either a Common-Era anchor (start_ce) OR an Anno Hegirae anchor (start_ah) OR an iso_start_date is required for a record to assert any temporality at all. Ranges are inclusive on both ends. The 'approximation' flag annotates the precision of the assertion (exact / circa / before / after / floruit / disputed).

start_ce.description: Start year in the Common Era (proleptic Gregorian). BCE years are encoded as negative integers (-44 for 44 BCE). Year 0 is permitted following ISO 8601 conventions and represents 1 BCE in proleptic Gregorian.

end_ce.description: End year (CE). MUST be >= start_ce when both are present (enforced by pipeline temporal validator, not by JSON Schema alone).

start_ah.description: Start year in Anno Hegirae (Islamic lunar calendar). 1 AH begins on 16 July 622 CE (Julian).

end_ah.description: End year (AH). MUST be >= start_ah when both are present.

iso_start_date.description: Precise start date in ISO 8601 format (YYYY-MM-DD). Use only when day-level precision is sourced; otherwise use start_ce / start_ah.

iso_end_date.description: Precise end date in ISO 8601 format.

approximation.description: Precision flag. 'exact' = year-precise from a credible source. 'circa' = approximate; record value as the conventional rounded year. 'before' = terminus ante quem; the actual date is at or before the recorded year. 'after' = terminus post quem. 'floruit' = the person/entity was active at this period (no precise birth/foundation date). 'disputed' = scholarly disagreement about the date.

uncertainty_years.description: Approximate margin of error in years. For 'circa' values, conventionally 25 (i.e., ±25 years); for 'floruit' often 50.

era_label.description: Human-readable period label, used as fallback for entities whose dates are vague (e.g., 'Late Umayyad', 'pre-Mongol', '5th–6th c. AH / 11th–12th c. CE').

owl_time_interval.description: URI reference to an OWL-Time time:ProperInterval individual, when one has been minted. Optional in Phase 0; pipeline auto-generates these in Phase 4 LOD export.

note.description: Free-text editorial note explaining the temporal assertion (e.g., 'Bosworth gives 749/132; Kennedy 750/132').'

$comment: Cross-field constraint (end >= start) is asserted in pipeline validators rather than via JSON Schema 'if/then' to keep the schema simple and human-auditable. Test fixtures in tests/fixtures/invalid/ contain a positive negative case (start > end) for which the pipeline validator must fail.
